title: The Docket - Week 5
subject: The quiet feature in GPT-5 that should terrify legal research vendors
preheader: Plus: why every firm needs a prompt library, and the discovery tool you're sleeping on
bodyHtml:
The Docket

LegalTech AI Hub · Week of May 6, 2026

OpenAI shipped a feature last week that barely made the news. It should have been front-page for anyone in legal tech. Let’s talk about it.

💣 GPT-5’s “Deep Research” Mode Now Cites Primary Sources

GPT-5’s latest update includes a “Deep Research” mode that browses the open web, pulls primary sources, and synthesizes them with citation links. In our testing, it found and correctly cited three appellate decisions, two federal regulations, and a state statute in under 30 seconds. Is it Westlaw? No. But it’s free, and it’s going to get a lot of lawyers asking why they’re paying $400/month for research tools. The legal research oligopoly is officially on notice.

📚 ABA Issues Updated Ethics Opinion on AI Use

The ABA’s Standing Committee on Ethics released Formal Opinion 523, clarifying that lawyers must understand the “material capabilities and limitations” of any AI tool they use. The key phrase: “A lawyer cannot delegate competence.” This means clicking “Accept” on AI output without substantive review may constitute a competence violation. It’s the clearest statement yet from the ABA. Read our breakdown.

🏢 Dentons Launches Firm-Wide AI Platform

Dentons rolled out a proprietary AI platform to all 12,000+ lawyers across 80 offices. It’s not a ChatGPT wrapper — it’s built on a fine-tuned model trained on the firm’s own precedent library (with client consent). This is the first global firm to deploy AI at this scale with firm-specific training data. Others are watching closely.

📣 Every Firm Needs a Prompt Library (Seriously)

We’re hearing from firms that their biggest AI bottleneck isn’t the technology — it’s that every lawyer is prompting differently. A shared prompt library, reviewed and refined by the team, is the simplest force multiplier in legal AI. One litigation boutique told us their prompt library cut AI rework by 60%. We’re building a starter template — check our guides page.

Tool Spotlight

Argument Gap Analyzer

Before you file that motion, run it through our Argument Gap Analyzer. It identifies logical weaknesses, unsupported assertions, and counterarguments the other side is likely to raise. Think of it as a moot court partner that never gets tired.

Try It Free →

See you next Wednesday. Build that prompt library this week — future you will thank present you.
